sn46.26:1.1: “Monks, develop the path & practice leading to the ending of craving.
sn46.26:1.2: And which is the path, which is the practice, leading to the ending of craving?
sn46.26:1.6: mindfulness as a factor for awakening, analysis of qualities as a factor for awakening, persistence as a factor for awakening, rapture as a factor for awakening, calm as a factor for awakening, concentration as a factor for awakening, & equanimity as a factor for awakening.
sn46.26:1.7: “When this was said, Ven. Udāyin said to the Blessed One,
sn46.26:1.8: “How are the seven factors for awakening developed, how are they pursued, so as to lead to the ending of craving?”
sn46.26:2.1: “There is the case, Udāyin, where a monk develops mindfulness as a factor for awakening dependent on seclusion, dependent on dispassion, dependent on cessation, abundant, enlarged, immeasurable, without ill will.
sn46.26:2.2: In him—as mindfulness as a factor for awakening is developed dependent on seclusion, dependent on dispassion, dependent on cessation, abundant, enlarged, immeasurable, without ill will—craving is abandoned. [Similarly with the remaining factors for awakening.]
sn46.26:2.3: “From the abandoning of craving, action [kamma] is abandoned.
sn46.26:2.4: From the abandoning of action, stress is abandoned.
sn46.26:2.9: “Thus, Udāyin, from the ending of craving comes the ending of action; from the ending of action, the ending of stress.”
